locations.name: All sectors
locations.description: Operating zone of Guild of Engineers. One of the Hegemonic High Guilds, responsible for resource acquisition, cybernetics, AI, tech advancement, and research.

locations.name: Orbiting incredibly close to a star (The Way of Light battle cruiser)
locations.description: Operating zone of Church of Stellar Flame. One of the official Hegemonic Cults.

locations.name: Warren (currency exchange HQ), shadow repositories sector-wide
locations.description: Operating zone of Counters Guild. Officials who maintain the galactic currency network and build shadow repositories in any system the Guild has a presence in, storing mysterious items and securing auctions and commerce.

locations.name: All sectors (intelligence network)
locations.description: Operating zone of Starless Veil. Hegemonic counterintelligence and spies.

locations.name: The Scorpio (dreadnought HQ), naval yards throughout the sector
locations.description: Operating zone of 51st Legion. A faction of the Hegemonic military, preparing a coup.

locations.name: Warren (Governor's residence)
locations.description: Operating zone of House Malklaith. A powerful Noble House of the Hegemony that ostensibly owns the sector.

locations.name: Orbiting near the star in Brekk system
locations.description: Operating zone of Isotropa Max Secure. The most notorious prison system in the Procyon sector, housing the worst of the worst.

locations.name: All major stations (certification offices)
locations.description: Operating zone of Starsmiths Guild. Maintain the jumpgates and hyperspace lanes, and build ships.

locations.name: Small island research station on Mem (HQ), dig site on Shimaya
locations.description: Operating zone of Cult of the Seekers. Wandering mystics studying artifacts and exploring, looking to open the Hantu gate.

locations.name: All major stations (broadcast infrastructure)
locations.description: Operating zone of Hegemonic News Network. Those who control the media control the mind.

locations.name: Deep space, Aleph system borders
locations.description: Operating zone of Yaru (Makers Guild). Guild that force-grows clones for labor.

locations.name: No fixed turf; The Grail (a bar on Sonhandra) as message drop
locations.description: Operating zone of Concordiat Knights. Fourth and fifth children of Noble Houses who have taken an oath sanctioned by the Hegemon to seek the Light of the World.

locations.name: Deep space, merchant routes throughout Procyon
locations.description: Operating zone of Sah'iir. Tall, ebon-skinned xenos who travel with blindfolded servants that speak for them.

locations.name: Unknown (research cells throughout sector)
locations.description: Operating zone of Suneaters. Ur-archaeologists and scientists obsessed with recreating jumpgate technology.

locations.name: Platform orbiting Planet Omega (HQ), secret chantries on Mem, Sonhandra, and Lithios
locations.description: Operating zone of The Agony. Human Cultists infecting themselves with Way creatures to access the universe in unsettling ways.

locations.name: Undocumented Ur ruin on Lithios, moon base on a Nightfall moon (HQ)
locations.description: Operating zone of Ashtari Cult. A Cult of Precursor worshipers claiming Ur descent.

locations.name: All sectors (disguised as ordinary citizens)
locations.description: Operating zone of Vignerons. A small handful of immortality seekers using artifact tech implants and chemicals distilled from the living.

locations.name: The Skeleton Key (ghost ship, location unknown)
locations.description: Operating zone of Ghosts. Scientists who, due to a mishap, live exo-suited in a half-phased state.

locations.name: Wandering (no fixed base)
locations.description: Operating zone of Mendicants. Originally the Church of the Emerald Heart, their organization was politically destroyed.

locations.name: Blackstarr (vast Nightspeaker ship), hidden conclave in Brekk
locations.description: Operating zone of Nightspeakers. Mystics with dark proclivities bent on finding a set of dangerous Precursor artifacts.

locations.name: Numerous meditation rooms throughout Indri
locations.description: Operating zone of Acolytes of Brashkadesh. A collective that eschews individuality.

locations.name: Secret bases on Baftoma and the Indri Wastelands (HQ)
locations.description: Operating zone of Conclave 01. Independent, sentient Urbots led by an ancient Urbot known as the Prime, seeking free will and independence for all Urbots.

locations.name: Outer system (mobile fleet)
locations.description: Operating zone of Lost Legion. Formerly the Hegemon's personal guard, they rebelled when the current Hegemon rose to power.

locations.name: All sectors (no fixed base)
locations.description: Operating zone of Scarlet Wolves. Although they often hire themselves out as bounty hunters, the Scarlet Wolves are a renowned group of assassins.

locations.name: All sectors (can access any terminal)
locations.description: Operating zone of Vorex. The most successful information broker to ever live.

locations.name: Drug dens, gambling houses, hidden reinforced bunker on Warren (HQ)
locations.description: Operating zone of Ashen Knives. Once lean and battle ready, the Ashen Knives are a decadent Syndicate focused on drugs, gambling, and pleasures of the flesh.

locations.name: Former Counters Guild shadow repository (HQ), Warren
locations.description: Operating zone of Borniko Syndicate. A tightly knit group of thieves who steal high-end technological supplies.

locations.name: Abandoned mining station in an Iota asteroid belt (HQ)
locations.description: Operating zone of Draxler's Raiders. Violent pirates who disable ships before boarding, ransoming crew and cargo alike.

locations.name: Nebula (difficult to navigate, exact location unknown)
locations.description: Operating zone of The Maelstrom. Rowdy space pirates living in a nebula that's difficult to navigate.

locations.name: Bright Wind gas cloud in Brekk system (racing grounds)
locations.description: Operating zone of Echo Wave Riders. Pilots.

locations.name: All sectors (mobile dealing operations)
locations.description: Operating zone of Janus Syndicate. Weapons dealers that specialize in ship weapons, headed up by the ruthless Viktor Bax, who insists on the first deal with every client in person.

locations.name: Holt system (drug den network)
locations.description: Operating zone of Turner Society. A Holt-based Syndicate running drug dens masquerading as society houses.

locations.name: The Pit mining quarry on Aleph (HQ), major berth and docks on Warren
locations.description: Operating zone of Cobalt Syndicate. Once a labor union, the Cobalt Syndicate has turned to smuggling and extortion to carve out shipping lanes and have a real say.

locations.name: Lost Paradise club with attached warehouse on Warren (HQ)
locations.description: Operating zone of Dyrinek Gang. Mostly young, disenfranchised xenos who have turned to crime and found strength and solidarity with each other.

locations.name: Brekk debris fields
locations.description: Operating zone of Wreckers. Scavengers and thieves with a few brilliant hackers, who incite factions to fight so that they may pick the battlefields clean later.
